prompt: Aerial oil painting, warm amber tones, cinematic overhead light. Dozens of small human clusters scattered across a vast plain. Each cluster has 6 to 8 figures huddled inward facing each other. At the edge of every single cluster, without exception, stands one figure who faces slightly outward — but that same figure is also touching the edge of another nearby cluster, where they are again the outermost person. No figure is ever at the center of anything. Every figure is simultaneously the most peripheral member of two different groups at once. The geometry makes this feel like a cosmic law, not a human tragedy. The mood is eerie and inevitable, not sad. Renaissance oil painting style. No text, no numbers, no lines, no nodes, no diagrams, no watermarks. Square format, 8K, museum quality.
visual_logic: Every figure occupies the periphery of its own cluster while bordering another, making centrality impossible for anyone — peripheral by geometry, not by choice.
